hints.Qui: 'Qui' is a relative pronoun meaning 'he who / whoever'
hints.duas: 'duas' is a numeral meaning 'two' in the accusative feminine
hints.tunicas: 'tunicas' is a noun meaning 'tunics' in the accusative plural

hints.Imperfect: Imperfect would be 'habebat' — 'habet' is present indicative

hints.habito, habitare, habitavi, habitatum: These are the parts of 'habitare' (to dwell) — derived from 'habere' but a different verb
hints.capio, capere, cepi, captum: These are the parts of 'capere' (to seize/take)
hints.teneo, tenere, tenui, tentum: These are the parts of 'tenere' (to hold)

hints.Nominative: Nominative plural would be 'tunicae' — 'tunicas' with -as ending is accusative plural
hints.Ablative: Ablative plural would be 'tunicis' — 'tunicas' is accusative
hints.Genitive: Genitive plural would be 'tunicarum' — 'tunicas' is accusative plural

hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'tunicae' ends in -ae
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'tunicae' ends in -ae
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'tunicae' ends in -ae

hints.Personal pronoun: Personal pronouns are 'ego, tu, is' — 'Qui' introduces a relative clause
hints.Demonstrative pronoun: Demonstrative pronouns are 'hic, ille, iste' — 'Qui' is relative
hints.Reflexive pronoun: Reflexive pronouns are 'se, sui' — 'Qui' is the relative pronoun
